result.stdout:
$USAGE: meg [--help] [--version] [env] [<subcommand> [<options>]]

SUBCOMMANDS:

    create <options>      Create the initial database.
    drop <options>        Drop the database.
    reset <options>       Drop and recreate the database.
    migrate <options>     Run migrations in the migrations directory
    gen <options>         Generate

    Use 'meg <subcommand> --help' for additional information.

OPTIONS:

    --version, -v         Print the version.
    env                   Print the meg environment.
    --help                display this list of options.

commands.payload:
$USAGE: meg [--help] [--version] [env] [<subcommand> [<options>]]

SUBCOMMANDS:

    create <options>      Create the initial database.
    drop <options>        Drop the database.
    reset <options>       Drop and recreate the database.
    migrate <options>     Run migrations in the migrations directory
    gen <options>         Generate

    Use 'meg <subcommand> --help' for additional information.

OPTIONS:

    --version, -v         Print the version.
    env                   Print the meg environment.
    --help                display this list of options.

result.stdout:
$USAGE: meg create [--help] [--db-name <db name>]
                  [--connection-string <connection string>]
                  [--provider <postgresql|mssql|mysql|sqlite>]

OPTIONS:

    --db-name, -d <db name>
                          Specify the name of the database to create. Defaults
                          to DB_NAME.
    --connection-string, -c <connection string>
                          Specify the admin database connection string. Must be
                          able to create DBs with the permissions of the user.
                          Defaults to DB_CONNECTION_STRING.
    --provider, -p <postgresql|mssql|mysql|sqlite>
                          Specify the database provider.
    --help                display this list of options.

result.stdout:
$USAGE: meg gen [--help] [<subcommand> [<options>]]

SUBCOMMANDS:

    migration <options>   Generate a new migration providing a schema.

    Use 'meg <subcommand> --help' for additional information.

OPTIONS:

    --help                display this list of options.

commands.payload:
$USAGE: meg gen [--help] [<subcommand> [<options>]]

SUBCOMMANDS:

    migration <options>   Generate a new migration providing a schema.

    Use 'meg <subcommand> --help' for additional information.

OPTIONS:

    --help                display this list of options.

result.stdout:
$USAGE: meg gen migration [--help] [--provider <postgresql|mssql|mysql|sqlite>]
                         [--migration-directory <migration directory>]
                         [<string>...]

SCHEMA DEFINITION:

    <string>...           Specify the schema definition of the migration in
                          this format:
                          -----------
                          meg gen migration AddUsersTable users Id:Id
                          Name:String Description:Text
                          -----------
                          List of all schema types for PostgreSQL (see your
                          provider by setting DB_PROVIDER) :
                          Id              an integer autoincrementing primary
                          key. Maps to INTEGER.
                          Guid            Sane default (uuid PK) for dotnet
                          guids. Maps to UUID.
                          Integer         Maps to INTEGER.
                          Float           Maps to FLOAT.
                          Numeric         Maps to NUMERIC.
                          Boolean         Maps to BOOLEAN.
                          String          Maps to VARCHAR(255).
                          Text            Maps to TEXT.
                          Binary          Maps to BYTEA.
                          Array           Maps to JSONB.
                          Record          Maps to JSONB.
                          Date            Maps to DATE.
                          Time            Maps to TIME.
                          DateTime        Timestamp without TZ. Maps to
                          TIMESTAMP.
                          DateTimeTz      Timestamp with TZ. Maps to TIMESTAMP.
                          -- References --
                          You can include references using
                          [ColumnName]:[ColumnType]:References:[ReferenceTableNa
                          me]:[ReferenceColumnName]
                          For example
                          -----------
                          user_id:int:references:users:id
                          -----------
                          -- Additional Properties --
                          You can include additional properties to a non
                          reference field using a similar syntax
                          [ColumnName]:[ColumnType]:[ADDITIONAL PROPERTIES]
                          For example
                          -----------
                          "username:string:unique not null"
                          -----------

OPTIONS:

    --provider, -p <postgresql|mssql|mysql|sqlite>
                          Specify the database provider. Defaults to
                          DB_PROVIDER.
    --migration-directory, -o <migration directory>
                          The output directory to write migrations to. When not
                          specified, the resulting SQL is written to env var
                          DB_MIGRATION_DIRECTORY value, defaults to 'Migrations'
    --help                display this list of options.

                         [--migration-directory <migration directory>]

                          Integer         Maps to INTEGER.
                          Float           Maps to FLOAT.

                          Text            Maps to TEXT.

                          Date            Maps to DATE.
                          Time            Maps to TIME.

                          me]:[ReferenceColumnName]

                          You can include additional properties to a non
                          reference field using a similar syntax

result.stdout:
$USAGE: meg migrate [--help] [--db-name <db name>]
                   [--connection-string <connection string>]
                   [--migration-directory <migration directory>]
                   [--provider <postgresql|mssql|mysql|sqlite>]

OPTIONS:

    --db-name, -d <db name>
                          Sepcify the database name. Defaults to DB_NAME.
    --connection-string, -c <connection string>
                          Specify the admin database connection string. Must be
                          able to create and update tables with the permissions
                          of the user. Defaults to DB_CONNECTION_STRING.
    --migration-directory, -i <migration directory>
                          Specify the directory that contains your order-named
                          migration .SQL files. Defaults to
                          DB_MIGRATION_DIRECTORY.
    --provider, -p <postgresql|mssql|mysql|sqlite>
                          Specify the database provider. Defaults to
                          DB_PROVIDER.
    --help                display this list of options.
